theme: The Office Has Memory — Every Moment Should Feel Earned, Connected, and Alive Over Time

ideas.title: Vibe Streak Counter — Reward Consistency
ideas.description: Track consecutive polling cycles where the team vibe stays the same using sessionStorage (key: 'bloberto-vibe-streak'). Store {vibe, count}. On each sync, increment if vibe matches, reset if it changes. When streak >= 3, show a small animated badge on the vibe pill in the header: '� 3-sync streak'. At 5+, swap to '⚡ On a roll'. At 10+, '💎 Legendary'. Animate the badge in with a quick scale bounce (keyframe: 0% scale(0), 70% scale(1.2), 100% scale(1)). WHY: Game mechanics like streaks create genuine investment. Users will leave the tab open just to watch the streak climb. It also contextualizes the vibe — a streak of 8 'crushing' syncs tells a story that a single data point never could.

ideas.title: Idle Duration Above Wandering Workers — Tell the Full Story
ideas.description: Idle workers are already rendered with the `char--idle` class and wander animations, but there's zero context about how long they've been idle. Add a small duration label above each idle character's nameplate using the worker's `updated_at` timestamp: format as '💤 8m' or '💤 1h'. In Office.jsx, pass the duration down to the Character component and render it in a `<div className='char__idle-duration'>` element (position: absolute, bottom: 100% of the char__name, font-size 0.6rem, color var(--text-muted), opacity 0.75). WHY: An idle worker who's been idle for 4 minutes is very different from one who's been idle 2 hours. Right now the office floor strips away all time context. Duration turns 'idle' from a status into a story — 'Sofia has been wandering for 47 minutes' immediately creates narrative tension.

ideas.title: Sync Countdown in the Live Indicator — Stop Leaving Users Guessing
ideas.description: The stats bar shows a 'Live' dot and last-sync time but no indication of when the next refresh will happen. Add a live countdown next to the sync timestamp: '↺ in 22s'. In App.jsx, track a `nextSyncIn` state initialized to POLL_INTERVAL_ACTIVE/1000. Decrement it with a setInterval(1000) alongside the existing poll timer. Reset to the appropriate interval after each sync. Render it in the stat-card as a small `↺ in {nextSyncIn}s` below the sync time. When count reaches 0, swap to '↺ Syncing…' while the fetch runs. WHY: Users who care enough to keep this tab open deserve to know when their data refreshes. The current UX creates anxiety: 'Is this stale? Is it stuck?' A simple countdown eliminates that entirely.

ideas.title: Deep Work Aura — Recognize the Long Haul
ideas.description: Any working worker who has been in 'working' status for 45+ minutes (check `updated_at` vs. Date.now()) deserves visual recognition. In Office.jsx, add a `char--deep-work` class to the Character when `(Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at)) > 45 * 60 * 1000`. In Office.css, add: `.char--deep-work .char__avatar { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 6px rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.7)); animation: deep-pulse 3s ease-in-out infinite; }` and `@keyframes deep-pulse { 0%,100% { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 5px rgba(251,191,36,0.5)); } 50% { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 12px rgba(251,191,36,0.9)); } }`. Also add a 🧘 label above their nameplate. On the Dashboard, show '🧘 Deep work' instead of the normal working duration when 45+ min. WHY: Long-running focus sessions are the most valuable thing in any engineering team. Right now they're invisible. A golden aura says: 'this person is in the zone — don't disturb.' It transforms duration into prestige.

ideas.title: Window Vibe-Weather Sync — The Sky Should Match the Mood
ideas.description: The window already shows time-of-day sky gradients. Layer vibe-reactive weather ON TOP of the time-based sky. This is purely additive — do not remove existing behavior. In WindowElement, receive a `vibeKey` prop (pass it from Office.jsx which already knows the vibe). Add conditional renders: vibe='on-fire' → show 2 small animated storm clouds (dark gray rounded divs) drifting slowly across the window, plus a lightning bolt pseudo-element flash every ~8s (CSS animation with 95% opacity 0, 96% opacity 1, 97% opacity 0 pattern). vibe='crushing' → add a simple SVG sun (circle with short radiating lines) in the top-right corner of the window sky that rotates slowly. vibe='slow-day' → a single flat gray cloud (no animation, just static). vibe='after-hours' already has star elements. WHY: The window is currently a passive time clock. Making it react to team state creates the feeling that the universe itself is responding to the team's work. A stormy window during 'on-fire' is instantly atmospheric and deeply satisfying.

ideas.title: Ghost Worker 'Available' Pulse — The Bench Should Feel Ready
ideas.description: Ghost workers (roster members not currently active) are rendered with the 'ghost' variant and appear faded at desks. They currently feel 'absent' or 'absent and forgotten'. Add a subtle soft green pulse to their avatar: `.char--ghost .char__avatar { animation: ghost-ready 4s ease-in-out infinite; opacity: 0.45; }` `@keyframes ghost-ready { 0%,100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(34,197,94,0); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 8px 2px rgba(34,197,94,0.25); } }`. Also change the ghost tooltip from just the role to: '${name} · Available'. WHY: Ghost workers are bench strength. They shouldn't feel like holes in the floor — they should feel like potential energy. A gentle green pulse says 'ready to go' rather than 'not here'. This reframes the team composition story entirely.

ideas.title: Office Snapshot to Clipboard — Make it Shareable
ideas.description: Add a '📸' button inside the office view (position: absolute, top-right of .office-wrap, small button with `btn btn-sm btn-ghost` styles). On click, use the native `html-to-image` npm package (or a lightweight canvas fallback using the existing SVG avatars + DOM layout) to capture .office-floor as a PNG. Use `navigator.clipboard.writeText` if Clipboard API isn't available for images, falling back to a `<a download>` trigger. Show a brief '✅ Copied!' toast (2s) after success. WHY: Shareability is the most powerful growth mechanic. When the whole team is crushing it, someone WILL want to screenshot that perfect office scene and drop it in the team Slack. Right now they have to manually screenshot. One click to share turns users into evangelists. This is how a dashboard becomes a product people talk about.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Daily Brief — A New Message Every Morning
ideas.description: Add a collapsible 'Daily Brief' section in the header (or as a dismissible banner just below the header). The message is determined by `new Date().toDateString()` hashed to an index in an array of 30+ unique 'manager memos' — guaranteeing a different one each calendar day. Examples: 'Memo: The CI pipeline is a suggestion, not a law.' / 'Today's theme: Aggressive optimism.' / 'Reminder: We pivot every sprint, not every commit.' Render as a small italic quote with a 📝 icon. Add a localStorage key 'bloberto-brief-dismissed-{date}' so users who dismiss it won't see it again that day. WHY: A daily changing message is the cheapest possible reason to open the tab again tomorrow. It creates a ritual — 'what did Bloberto say today?' — without any backend. Combined with the vibe streak, this turns a monitoring dashboard into something with personality and continuity.

ideas.title: Worker Personality-Based Idle Animations — Stop Making Everyone Wander the Same
ideas.description: Currently all idle workers use generic `char--wander-1` through `char--wander-4` keyframes that are essentially the same lateral drift. Each team member has a distinct SVG avatar with personality — Dave has headphones, Maya has pink hair, Sofia has a magnifying glass. Their idle animations should match. In Office.css, add worker-specific wander variants: Carlos: slightly wider X drift (methodical), Maya: add a 360deg spin halfway through the wander (impulsive), Dave: slow pace back and forth with almost no Y movement (stoic), Sofia: small figure-eight pattern (curious). Apply by adding `char--wander-carlos` etc. class based on `worker.id` in the Character component, falling back to the existing wander-N variants for unknown workers. WHY: Personality is the most memorable part of this dashboard. The moment users notice Dave pacing and Maya spinning, they will narrate it to colleagues. This is the difference between 'cool dashboard' and 'the one with the characters that act like themselves'.

ideas.title: Vibe History Sparkline — Memory for the Stats Bar
ideas.description: The stats bar shows a snapshot in time but has no memory. Add a tiny sparkline (10-point line chart, pure SVG, 60px wide × 20px tall) to the Team Vibe stat card that shows the last 10 recorded vibes as a line connecting 5 vibe levels mapped to Y values (crushing=5, in-flow=4, slow-day=3, on-fire=2, after-hours=1). On each successful sync, push the current vibe key to a sessionStorage array (max 10). On mount, read from sessionStorage to restore the spark. Render the SVG using a simple polyline calculated from the Y-mapped vibe values. Color the line with the current vibe color. WHY: A sparkline turns a single stat into a trend. 'Crushing It' as a current state is fine. 'Crushing It after 3 syncs of on-fire' is a comeback story. The sparkline makes that visible at a glance without taking any space from the existing card. This is the minimum viable team retrospective.

ideas.title: Plant Watering Easter Egg — The Office Has Secret Life
ideas.description: The decorative plants (there are 3 — two leaf plants and a cactus) currently exist solely as static SVG props. Add an onClick handler to each `.office-plant`. On click, trigger a 1.5s watering animation: spawn a tiny 💧 emoji that arcs from outside the floor and lands on the plant (CSS keyframe: start above-right with opacity 0, arc toward plant top, end at plant with opacity 1 then fade). Also briefly scale the plant to 110% and back (spring ease). Track 'total waters' in localStorage. At 10 total waters, the cactus sprouts a tiny SVG flower (a small pink circle added to its top, rendered conditionally). At 25 waters, a hidden `aria-label` on the plants changes to 'very happy plant'. WHY: Easter eggs that reward patience create devotion. The moment someone discovers the cactus bloomed after 10 clicks, they will tell everyone. It's the kind of thing that ends up in team lore. And it costs almost nothing — three click handlers and a couple keyframes.

ideas.title: Editable Office Sign — Let the Team Own It
ideas.description: The '🏢 Bloberto's HQ' office sign is currently hardcoded JSX. Make it editable: double-click the sign to replace it with an `<input>` (max 24 chars), styled identically to the sign (same font-size, font-weight, background transparent, border-bottom dashed 2px accent). On Enter or blur, save to localStorage as 'bloberto-office-name' and restore the static display. On mount, read from localStorage. Add a very faint '✏️' icon that appears on hover as a hint. WHY: Ownership is the most powerful engagement mechanic. The moment someone types their actual team name into that sign — 'Payments Team' or 'Studio B' or 'Chaos Corner' — they have claimed the dashboard. It stops being Bloberto's product and becomes theirs. This is also zero-backend, zero-config personalization, which is the holy grail.
